race.name: L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France
race.display_name: L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera
race.tagline: Experience the Tour de France atmosphere on fully closed roads in the Rhodope Mountains

climate.overview: Warm summer conditions typical of Bulgarian Rhodope Mountains in early July; expect temperatures 20-30°C with potential afternoon showers. Specific forecasts unavailable.

terrain.overview: Fully closed paved roads through Rhodope Mountains featuring sustained climbs, technical descents, and scenic reservoir views.

logistics.explanation: The event in Peshtera benefited from strong municipal support, meticulous route preparation, and safety measures, enabling smooth execution for 1,200 cyclists across multiple formats.[1][2] Minor deductions arise from potential first-time hosting challenges in the new location.

length.explanation: Offering 58 km and 112 km distances, the routes provide solid options but lack ultra-long challenges exceeding 150 km common in elite gran fondos.[1][2]

technicality.explanation: The circuit features winding ascents like the Saint Konstantin climb and varied terrain around Batak Lake, demanding precise handling and pacing skills.[2]

elevation.explanation: With 1,100 m for the short race and 2,200 m for the long, the climbs through the Rhodope Mountains deliver substantial vertical challenge without overwhelming amateurs.[1][2]

climate.explanation: August timing in Peshtera ensures mild summer weather in the Rhodope Mountains, ideal for endurance riding with low rain risk and comfortable temperatures.[2]

altitude.explanation: Primarily low-elevation routes starting in Peshtera and climbing to St. Konstantin resort, lacking high-altitude passes that test hypoxia adaptation.[1][2]

adventure.explanation: Riders experience stunning Rhodope scenery, historic Batak streets, serene Batak Lake shores, and a festive atmosphere, creating an unforgettable exploratory journey.[2]

prestige.explanation: Branded under Tour de France's L'Étape series with international draw from 16 countries, but as a third-edition amateur event in Bulgaria, it trails flagship Étape du Tour prestige.[1][5]

race_quality.explanation: Well-organized with diverse formats, challenging circuits, and jubilant crowds, elevated by Tour de France branding and pro-level route design.[2]

experience.explanation: Combines epic climbs, breathtaking views, family rides, live concerts, and a Tour museum, crafting a holistic festival of cycling passion and community.[1][2]

community.explanation: Peshtera's warm embrace, local engagement, and 1,200 riders from 16 nations fostered strong camaraderie and shared endurance spirit.[1][2]

field_depth.explanation: Attracts 1,200 amateurs from 16 countries, offering decent international variety but limited elite or pro-level depth typical of top gran fondos.[1]

value.explanation: Delivers high-quality Tour de France-branded challenge, scenery, and festivities at accessible amateur pricing in Bulgaria's scenic Rhodopes.[2]

expenses.explanation: Reasonable for the experience but travel to Peshtera and Rhodope lodging add costs for international participants from 16 countries.[1]

biased_opinion.logistics: 4 - Well-organized Tour de France franchise event with Race Village, though international travel logistics add complexity[1].
biased_opinion.length: 3 - Solid 102km long route but shorter than flagship Etape du Tour distances[1].
biased_opinion.technicality: 4 - Mountain descents near Batak Dam require solid bike handling skills[1].
biased_opinion.elevation: 4 - 2,500m gain over 102km provides serious climbing challenge for amateurs[1].
biased_opinion.climate: 4 - Favorable July weather in Rhodopes, warm but not extreme[1].
biased_opinion.altitude: 3 - Highest point 1,350m is challenging but not high-altitude suffering[1].
biased_opinion.adventure: 5 - Stunning Rhodope scenery, closed roads, reservoir views create epic adventure[1].
biased_opinion.prestige: 3 - Official Tour de France L'Étape Series event carries strong brand recognition[4].
biased_opinion.race_quality: 4 - Professional organization standards from Tour de France franchise[1][4].
biased_opinion.experience: 5 - Authentic Tour atmosphere with Race Village in mountain setting[1].
biased_opinion.community: 4 - Growing regional cycling scene boosted by Tour de France branding[1].
biased_opinion.field_depth: 3 - Amateur sportive with competitive long route but not elite field[1].
biased_opinion.value: 4 - Premium Tour de France experience at accessible Eastern Europe pricing[1].
biased_opinion.expenses: 3 - International travel from Western Europe adds cost; local value excellent
biased_opinion.cultural_impact: 2 - Young event (4th edition 2026) building local cycling culture[1][4]

final_verdict.one_liner: T2 - Premium Tour de France franchise sportive delivering authentic Gran Fondo experience in spectacular Rhodope Mountains. Excellent for climbers seeking closed-road adventure.

logistics.overview: Start/finish in Peshtera city center. Fully closed roads. Race Village provides Tour de France atmosphere. International travelers should fly into Sofia (2.5hr drive). Early registration recommended[1].

history.overview: Launched January 10, 2023 as inaugural edition. 2026 marks 4th edition (2023-2026). Part of global L'Étape Series across 20+ countries[1][4].

course_description.character: Start Peshtera city center → climb through town → **SUFFERING ZONE 1: Sustained ascent to Sveti Konstantin resort (1,350m highest point)** → scenic descent past Batak Dam → through Batak town → **FINAL SUFFERING ZONE: City center finish circuits**. Fully closed roads showcase Rhodope beauty[1].

guide_variables.signature_feature: Tour de France branding on closed Rhodope roads

non_negotiables: Book early - Tour de France events sell out
Climbing legs required for long route
Mountain bike handling skills for descents
